title: Brennan2019: EEG during Naturalistic Listening to Alice in Wonderland
description: This dataset comprises scalp EEG recordings from 45 participants during passive listening to a naturalistic audiobook excerpt (first chapter of Alice's Adventures in Wonderland, ~12.4 minutes). The study investigates how the brain constructs hierarchical syntactic structure and generates rapid linguistic predictions during continuous speech comprehension. Each recording is aligned with word-by-word linguistic annotations including lexical frequency, acoustic features, and computational surprisal measures from n-gram, recurrent neural network, and context-free grammar models.
methods_description: EEG was recorded using a 61-channel BrainVision actiCHamp amplifier with an equidistant montage (easyCAP M10), yielding 60 scalp channels plus 1 bipolar vertical EOG and 1 audio channel. Sampling rate was 500 Hz with online filtering (0.01–200 Hz). Participants listened to the audiobook stimulus presented via insert earphones (Etymotic ER-2) at 45 dB above individual hearing threshold. Data were collected between March 2015 and December 2016 at the University of Michigan Computational Neurolinguistics Lab.
